Healthcare Remote Jobs Supporting Patient Care From Anywhere

If you’re a healthcare professional considering a change, remote jobs might offer you the flexibility you’ve been seeking. Working from home now extends far beyond traditional roles, from telehealth appointments to behind-the-scenes operations. You could find yourself making a meaningful impact on patient care—no matter your location. With technology opening new doors, you’ll soon discover how these roles are shaping the future of the industry and what that could mean for you next.

The Evolution of Remote Work in Healthcare

The landscape of healthcare employment has undergone notable changes in recent years, particularly in the aftermath of the COVID-19 pandemic. As one explores job opportunities or joins talent communities within the sector, there is an observable increase in available remote positions across various roles, such as Clinical Analysts and software engineers. This shift reflects healthcare organizations' efforts to deliver services to patients regardless of geographic location.

The move towards remote work in healthcare presents opportunities for career advancement, including various job openings and internship programs. These roles often incorporate a level of flexibility that can enhance work-life balance while still adhering to stringent standards for personal information protection and regulatory compliance.

Our Story

Alberta Chicken Producers is a provincial 'farmer-run' organization representing approximately 250 chicken farmers in Alberta. 

Our farmers are local families who are passionately committed to providing consumers with safe, high-quality, locally produced chicken—raised under the highest standards of animal care and food safety.
100% of chicken farmers in Alberta are certified under the national Animal Care Program and On-Farm Food Safety Program as a condition of their licence to market chicken.
Our farms are audited and certified annually by third-party auditors.

Our industry is growing sustainably.

Careers
Careers in Massage Therapy at Align

Align was founded in 2000 and has organically grown to a group of 8 locations in Ottawa.  After 25 years, we've got the business side down pat, and welcome new RMTs and experienced ones to join us and lose the stress. 




We have everything you require to start, grow and maintain a practice - receptionists, linen service, marketing - social media and radio ads, online booking and charting, tools and training and a competitive split. 

We don't have crappy pay (We love writing big cheques!), rigid scheduling, micromanagers or scary contracts. Most therapists start and stay with us, and move on if they literally move (to another city usually). 




Align is by far the largest employer of massage therapists in the city (1/5), and with good reason. We are a team, a group of friends and would love to meet you!




Currently, we are looking for full or part-time RMTs for Kanata, Rockland, Bank St, Barrhaven, Lindenlea, and Sandy Hill. 




Are you an RMT looking to open a clinic and unsure where to start? We are always looking for keeners to help grow the brand, so please reach out and we can show you how much easier it is working in a team.


Please send a resume, or pop in for a visit! You can reach the head honcho at jana@alignyourbody.com

Customer service and cash

A culture with purpose

Working at Atuka means contributing everyday to the development trajectories of potentially life-changing therapeutics, supported by world-class facilities and scientists, and an industry-leading benefits plan.

Atuka offers a way of doing science that is different from traditional contract research organizations.

Every day, we support our partners in making informed, critical decisions at the interface between the preclinical and the clinical, ensuring that future research and investment is directed toward the avenues with the best chance of success.

At the same time, our culture remains very much influenced by our roots in academic research and experimentation, and we are committed to ongoing scientific discovery in Parkinson’s and other disorders.

LIFE AT ATUKA

Atuka is a place for scientists who want their work to matter. See how we work, grow, and collaborate to advance the next generation of Parkinson’s therapies.

Download our Culture Book
At Atuka we embrace our role in nurturing and preparing the next generation of scientists, technicians, and support teams for the crucial challenges that lay ahead. 

Work alongside a team of leading neuroscientists with numerous research breakthroughs to their credit.

Join a culture that values collaboration, scientific inquiry, and continuous learning.

Define the career path most meaningful, interesting, and rewarding to you.

Careers and Articling Opportunities at MGL Lawyers
At MGL Lawyers, we value giving articling students hands-on practical experience to set them up for success in their legal careers. Our articling program is directly supervised by our lawyers and you will have exposure to the entire team and various legal matters.
Why Article at MGL Lawyers?

As an articling student, you will be exposed to a wide range of immigration matters. There will also be opportunities to gain exposure in business law and in litigation matters. You will work with our team of lawyers in drafting, preparing immigration applications, legal opinions, attending meetings with our lawyers, and contributing to strategy.  

Our senior lawyers are dedicated to providing mentorship, guidance and support, helping you develop skills and confidence throughout your articling term.

Our collaborative approach and inclusive environment are at the heart of everything we do. You’ll work closely with lawyers and our team throughout your articling term.

We offer opportunities for training, development and networking, including workshops, seminars and legal community events.

Who are we looking for?

The firm seeks graduates and students who are resourceful, have a go-getter attitude, and can work in a collaborative team environment. An interest and passion for immigration is considered an asset when applying to our team.

Whether you’re passionate about community engagement, advocacy, or project management, Community Renewal offers opportunities for growth and learning.

Careers in Trucking
A rewarding career in trucking is the next destination.

As the backbone of the Canadian economy, the trucking industry offers diverse career paths, competitive salaries, and unparalleled opportunities for growth. With nearly 300,000 drivers and 450,000 Canadians employed overall, generating $24 billion in personal income.

The Road to Success

The Canadian trucking industry is essential to our economy and will only become more critical in the years to come. This translates to long-term career opportunities for those who choose this dynamic industry.

Rewarding

The trucking industry offers competitive pay and benefits packages, with opportunities for increased earning potential based on experience and skills. Whether a driver, mechanic, or logistics coordinator, teams can expect to earn a good living and receive comprehensive benefits.

Growth

As an essential sector of the Canadian economy, trucking provides security and opportunities advancement. With an increasing demand for skilled professionals, individuals can build a stable and rewarding career in trucking, with opportunities to move into leadership roles.

Driven by a pioneering spirit, we’ve grown from our humble beginnings of just 15 small utility trailers to now serving more than two million customers worldwide. Our diverse operating companies have withstood economic changes, giving us the strength and stability to seize opportunities and continue to innovate. 

Join us and help tell our tale.

Camille Breton Skagen

Operations Manager

Camille holds a Master in Sociology from the University of British Columbia and has completed a wide array of professional development training, with a focus on effective leadership, conflict resolution and communication. An experienced project manager for a diverse range of startups and community based projects, Camille most recently served as project manager for a large scale clinical checklist initiative. Run in association with the Ontario Incidence Study of Reported Child Abuse and Neglect, the project responded to the needs of child welfare-involved families during the COVID-19 pandemic. Since 2020, Camille has been the project manager at the League for both the Beyond Neglect project and the Strength of Families and Connection initiative. She is known for her innovative vision, her leadership skills and her facility in building strong working relationships.

Job Description/Responsibilities: 

  

The Ecological Field Technician will provide support for a range of programs for the Clean Annapolis River Project aimed at supporting ecosystem health, biodiversity and climate resilience in southwest Nova Scotia through collaboration with the agricultural sector, government partners, academic institutions, community organizations, and individual residents. Discover Why People Love Working at Comfort Keepers®
Caregiver

For more than 20 years, Comfort Keepers® has provided the highest quality in-home care to those who need a hand with daily life and activities. Aftercare training (including infection control training), our PSWs travel to clients’ homes to help seniors achieve a greater quality of life through home care, and companionship, and provide moments of joy whether big or small.

Our personal support workers love working at Comfort Keepers. Read our caregiver testimonials to find out why.

A typical Caregiver job description may include:
Meal preparation and cooking
Conversation and companionship
Light housekeeping
Transportation to and from doctor’s appointments (or arranging telemedicine appointments)
Running Errands (or arranging grocery delivery)
Helping with personal care, including bathing, dressing, hygiene
Reminding the client to take their medication on time
Helping with feeding and other everyday tasks
Tracking your client’s condition and reporting changes to your supervisor
Assisting with Alzheimer’s Care
Offering respite care to family caregivers

Our Mission

To empower healthcare providers to serve their residents better.

Our Vision

Quick, easy, secure, and barrier-free access to health care resources for everyone.

Our Values
Innovation

We are constanlty innovating on what matters most to our customers. We are scrappy, audacious, and committed to digitizing healthcare.

Transparency

We foster a culture of openness with our team and customers. We welcome differing opinions, and believe in "disagree and commit" to move forward.

Empowerment

Everything we do is to empower our customers to excel at their jobs, while continuing to challenge ourselves to grow and improve every day.

Pragmatic & Swift

We are pragmatic problem solvers. We root decisions in data, logic, and intuition, while valuing speed and action.

Our story

Evoke Health is the brainchild of two engineers, Varsha Chaugai and Graham Fraser, founded on a simple yet powerful belief: that healthcare should be accessible, transparent, and empowering for patients, families, and care teams alike.

What is the Federation of Law Societies of Canada?

The Federation of Law Societies of Canada is the national association of the 14 law societies mandated by the provinces and territories to regulate Canada’s legal profession in the public interest. It is the body through which Canada’s law societies collaborate at the national level, share information on trends and issues affecting the legal profession, and engage in collective action and decision-making. The Federation is also the law societies’ national and international voice on important issues related to the regulation and core values of the legal profession.

Leader in Free Access to Legal Information
Certifying Body of Internationally Educated Lawyers and Law Graduates

National Voice of Canada’s Law Societies
Our Vision

Acting in the public interest by strengthening Canada’s system of governance of an independent legal profession, reinforcing public confidence in it and making it a leading example for justice systems around the world.

VOLUNTEER OPPORTUNITIES

Archiving Goulbourn:

The Museum is enhancing the accessibility of its collections by transcribing historic handwritten documents and manuscripts.

Our current project focuses on a Goulbourn Township Council meeting minutes book, with records dating back to 1899. This document holds special significance, as it was originally written in the very building that now houses the Museum, which once served as the Township Hall.

This volunteer opportunity is fully remote and hosted through the Museum’s Google Workspace account. Volunteers are given access to a shared folder containing transcription assignments and use a Google Sheets template to record the transcribed text.

As an Archiving Goulbourn volunteer, you can set your own schedule, contributing as much time as you wish to this meaningful preservation project.

For more information about this volunteer opportunity please email info@goulbournmuseum.ca

Start a Career Working in a Specialized Hand Therapy Rehab Clinic Now

Join the staff at the Hand and Upper Limb Clinic for the opportunity to be a part of an amazing, supportive and talented group of individuals in an environment that promotes good health in all aspects. We treat a variety of patients including: post-operative surgical patients, patients with moderate to severe injuries of the hand and upper limb, patients with arthritic diagnoses or motor neuron conditions, and more.

Acquire valuable experience working in the foremost shoulder rehab clinic in north-western Ontario.

Currently, we require a full-time Physiotherapist; outpatient orthopedic experience is an asset (not necessarily in the upper extremity). This will be a salaried position including paid vacation and extended health benefits, depending upon experience. Preference for CHT.

We are also seeking a Registered Massage Therapist with either full or part-time availability.

Apply now and begin a rewarding career in private health care!

Part-time positions for provincially licensed physicians available in a busy urban clinic specializing in men’s sexual health testing, diagnosis and treatment. Day, evening and weekend clinics available.

Being Part of Invixium Means Making an Impact Every Day

As a member of our team, you’ll take ownership for a piece of our bold vision for the future. Working with us means taking on dynamic, exciting challenges every day with an insatiable desire to exceed your goals.

We’re looking for creative minds with a hunger to make a difference in the world. You are ambitious, full of passion, and bring limitless energy to everything you do. You don’t just think outside the box – you reinvent it.

Join some of the biometric industry’s leading innovators in improving how the world works. At Invixium, design is as much of our DNA as biometrics, so we have built an all-star team of creative thinkers, scientific achievers and passionate leaders to achieve excellence in our field. Invixium is more than a security company. We are leaders in design, technology, user experience and customer service.

We’re motivated by one unifying goal: become the most respected name in biometric security. If that sounds exciting, we want to get to know you.

We provide individualized, evidence-based support for children with learning and developmental needs. We focus on targeted interventions and accommodations that build academic skills, confidence, and independence. Our services include ongoing learning support and skill building, and, when appropriate, psychoeducational assessments to better understand each child’s strengths and challenges.

It is not too late to sign up to keep that brain fresh all summer, in a fun, engaging environment.

Summer Learning! Intakes Open! Catch up, keep up or reach ahead!

Click the 'Book Consultation' button below, and we will contact you to learn more about your child.

Book a Consultation

Our services are offered Monday to Sunday, and are supervised by Registered School and Clinical Psychologist, Dr. Rebecca Moore. Not only does this mean your child’s progress and program is monitored by our psychologist, it also means that all of our services are claimable under most benefit plans.

We offer education services Mondays through Friday from 8am - 8pm as well as Saturday and Sunday 8am - 3pm.

All of our full services are available as in person sessions, or remote sessions.

We're Building Something Real

Our Team is Building the Future, Remotely and On-Site

We’re engineers, gamer geeks, aviation nerds, and drone enthusiasts. The individuals who are building the future here have travelled many paths - but what we all have in common is the ability to thrive on change; a desire to work at something meaningful; and an unquenchable need to invent the future in our own image. This is Kongsberg Geospatial.


Current Job Listings

We Build Meaningful Technology


Kongsberg Geospatial creates real-time geospatial technology that powers life-protecting defense systems and unmanned aerial systems worldwide - systems that do such things as deliver defibrillators to people in need and help search and rescue operations in the Arctic.

People rely on our technology for a wide range of applications including air defense, search and rescue, drone delivery systems, and autonomous vehicle tracking.

Our team members make a real impact in people's lives.


We’re expanding our team, and we’re looking for people who want to leave their mark on the world.
‍


Take Control of Your Work Environment

Our team members have the opportunity for real input into product direction. Our engineers enjoy technical autonomy and get to design the software they build. We create high-performance technology with real-world applications. Join us, and make a difference.

INTERESTED IN JOINING LAYWINE’S?




At Laywine’s, we are always interested in hearing from high-calibre people who share our curiosity, care, and respect for well-made things. Ours is a business built on knowledge, thoughtful service, and genuine human connection—whether that’s helping a customer choose their first fountain pen or discussing the finer points of paper, leather, craftsmanship, or design.

We look for people who are engaged, detail-oriented, and generous with their expertise. If you take pride in doing things properly, enjoy learning and sharing what you know, and believe that great retail is as much about listening as it is about selling, we would love to hear from you.

Even if there isn’t a role currently advertised, we welcome thoughtful introductions from those who feel they would be a good fit for the Laywine’s team.

Senior Leasing Manager (Commercial Real Estate)

Position Summary

Reporting to the SVP, Leasing, the Senior Leasing Manager is responsible for driving leasing performance across a diversified portfolio of retail and mixed-use assets, including fund-owned investments, core income-producing properties, and select development projects.

The role focuses on maximizing asset value through strategic leasing, tenant curation, and disciplined deal execution, while supporting both portfolio-level performance and investor and ownership objectives where applicable.

This position operates at the intersection of leasing, asset management, and investment strategy, ensuring leasing decisions enhance income stability, long-term value, and overall portfolio performance across different ownership structures.

This position offers exposure to both fund-driven investments and directly managed operating assets, providing a broader and more hands-on leasing platform.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and execute leasing strategies aligned with asset business plans and portfolio and investment performance targets

Source, negotiate, and complete lease transactions that enhance asset value and long-term income stability

Structure deals to optimize net effective rent, tenant quality, and risk-adjusted returns

Speaker: Dr. Jon Thomas, Director, Esposito Family Centre for Innovation & Entrepreneurship, University of the Fraser Valley

Dr. Jon Thomas is the Director of the Esposito Family Centre for Innovation & Entrepreneurship at the University of the Fraser Valley. He holds the BC Regional Innovation Chair in Canada-India Partnership Development at UFV and is an Associate Professor in Innovation & Entrepreneurship at the School of Business, UFV. He is also a Visiting Associate Professor at SFU’s Beedie School of Business.


Dr. Thomas has guided scientists and engineers commercializing their inventions for social impact for many years. He teaches in the Invention to Innovation (i2I) program at Beedie and has taught Managing Technological Innovation in the SFU Management of Technology (MOT) MBA program.


Dr. Thomas holds a Ph.D. in Technology Management & Strategy from IIT Delhi and has been a Mitacs Elevate postdoctoral fellow examining innovation in the nanobiotechnology industry sponsored by SFU and CDRD Ventures Inc. We are seeking a Doctor of Chiropractic for our Christian, family wellness, subluxation based, corrective care Chiropractic office, located on the east side of Ottawa in Orléans, Ontario. I have cared for many people in the past 25 years, with a focus on spinal correction using the latest rehabilitation techniques. Through the pandemic to present day, I continue to have high new patient numbers. I am equipped with digital x-ray and PostureRay digitizing software to track structural change. If you want to learn how to correct the structure of the spine and impact the health of people, this is a great opportunity. I am ready to mentor a chiropractor who is willing to learn and grow.

Candidates must have a Doctor of Chiropractic degree.
Candidates must be registered with the College of Chiropractors of Ontario.
Candidates must be legally permitted to work in Canada.
French is an asset in our bilingual community, but not required.

DOCTOR OF CHIROPRACTIC – INDEPENDENT CHIROPRACTOR

I am seeking a Chiropractor who wants to create their dream practice as an independent contractor, leasing my office on my off days. Live to Play Sports is actively monitoring the progression of the coronavirus, COVID-19, to ensure that we are following updates and procedures from the government and public health. Any offer of employment will be made conditional upon the person providing proof of full COVID-19 vaccination or proof of an applicable exemption under human rights law.

For over 50 years, we have been a leading player in the Canadian and international bicycle market. Starting as a one-man operation in small-town British Columbia, we have always stayed true to our roots, which has helped us grow. We now have three international offices, two distribution centers, and more than 100 dedicated, fantastic staff that contribute to our continued success in the cycling industry.

We work hard, but we ride hard too. At Live to Play Sports, we have bicycle storage, showers, and lockers to encourage riding to, from, and at work. There is almost always someone getting out for a pedal, whether it is road, mountain, dirt jump, or a cruise. If you want to work in a casual environment with a dedicated and passionate culture where people love the outdoors, then we'd love to hear from you.

Energy industry veterans lead Braya, lending the project their expertise in refining and biofuels operations, trading, and logistics. By harnessing this wealth of talent and knowledge, Braya is in a unique position to take advantage of the exciting opportunities of the renewable energy sector.

Requirements : The Orleans Urgent care Clinic (OUCC) employs physicians with a fellowship or certification in Emergency Medicine or physicians with sufficient experience in emergency medicine. Because we are a fully equipped emergency facility, our physicians must have the appropriate training and experience. Many of our physicians continue to work in local Emergency Departments.

Employment Arrangement : Physicians may become OUCC associates however there are  also non associate physicians who work casual hours.

Hours : Physician weekday work hours are 8 a.m. to 3 p.m.,  9 a.m. to 4 p.m., and 3 p.m. to 9 p.m. Weekend hours are 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., and 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. The OUCC clinic is closed on statutory holidays.

Salaries : Physicians bill OHIP for insured services, and standard OMA rates are billed for uninsured services.

Nursing

Requirements : In keeping with the Orleans Urgent care Clinic’s high standard of emergency care, we hire experienced Registered Nurses (RN) and Registered Practical Nurses (RPN).

Employment Arrangement : Scheduling is managed by the Clinic’s nursing team leader. Schedules are distributed months in advance.

Hours : Weekday nursing hours are from 8:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m., and from 3:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. Weekend hours are from 8:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.

Salaries : OUCC nurses are paid competitive hourly rates.

About ORC

The Ottawa Rowing Club (ORC) is a not-for-profit sports organization established in 1867 to provide rowing instruction for participants of all ages.  The ORC is comprised of a volunteer Executive Committee, volunteers and paid employees. 

The ORC provides coaching to rowers in competitive or sport rowing programs, as well as introductions to the sport through Discover Rowing Youth Summer Camps, Adult Learn-to-Row, and the Adult Rowing League.  Local high schools and universities train and race out of the ORC.

Our Club is located at 10 Lady Grey Dr, in the heart of Downtown Ottawa. Our Club is composed of 2 large boathouses, that include an indoor fitness area, offices, board room, boat storage, a ballroom, kitchenette, washrooms and change rooms. The Club is right on the edge of the Ottawa river with impressive panoramic views blending rural rugged shoreline with modern urban architecture.

At ORC we work in support of a diverse and equal world. We welcome all diverse people into our already eclectic teams. We encourage People of Colour, Indigenous Peoples, people from the LGBTQ+ community as well as people with Disabilities to apply!

Peacekeeping Career: How to Start and What to Expect

Want a career that matters? Peacekeeping combines real-world impact with tough challenges. If you're curious about what's needed and how to begin, this page gives clear steps, real roles, and practical tips.

What peacekeeping jobs look like

Peacekeeping jobs vary. You can be a military officer, police officer, medic, logistician, civil affairs officer, human rights officer, or translator. Many missions also need engineers, IT specialists, and administrators. Civilian roles include political affairs, rule-of-law advisors, and community liaison officers. Short-term contract work and long-term positions both exist.

Skills and qualifications

Employers look for relevant education plus field experience. A degree in international relations, law, public health, engineering, or logistics helps. Practical experience through NGOs, national militaries, or police forces is very valuable. Learn a UN language: French, Spanish, Arabic, Russian, or Chinese can make you stand out, but English is usually required. Take courses in first aid, conflict analysis, protection of civilians, and humanitarian training. Mental resilience, cultural sensitivity, and basic medical knowledge matter as much as formal degrees.

Start with self-assessment: list your skills, languages, and physical readiness. Volunteer locally with refugee centers, NGOs, or disaster relief groups to get hands-on experience. Apply for short-term m

Our Why = Your Why

We think our beliefs are the same as yours—that all are worthy of connectedness, companionship, and community.

What Is an Intellectual Disability?

An intellectual disability begins before adulthood and is a significantly reduced ability to:

Understand new or complex information
Learn and apply new skills

Signs of the disability often appear during childhood. It is a lifelong experience, with most people needing some type of assistance throughout their lives to live in and be part of their community.

Disability Service Sector Goals

We are aiming to raise awareness about the Disability Service sector and its workforce of over 4600 employees supporting 6500 people experiencing intellectual disabilities across Saskatchewan. It’s about getting to know us and being part of ensuring inclusive communities for people experiencing disabilities, and it all starts with saying hello.

The Disability Service sector’s ultimate goal is for people experiencing intellectual disabilities to live healthy, safe, and meaningful lives in their community.

Importance of Community Support

People experiencing intellectual disabilities often require support as part of daily life.

The amount of support depends on the extent of the disability
Some people may need to learn skills to live independently
Others may require 24/7 one-on-one support

Employment with Children’s Treatment Network

Children’s Treatment Network (CTN) supports approximately 42,000 kids and youth with disabilities and other developmental needs and their families. We provide intake, service navigation and coordinated service planning, assessment and diagnostic services, specialized clinics and rehabilitation services, including physiotherapy, occupational therapy and speech language therapy.

Funded by the Ministry of Children, Community and Social Services, CTN offers services through contracted partner organizations in the education, health and community sectors. Through its innovative network model, CTN’s commitment to providing family-centred care is anchored by an electronic client record that is shared across its partner organizations and provides the foundation for integrated plans of care and services.

Dedicated to creating a vibrant community where all kids, youth and families belong, we have a strong commitment to equity, diversity, inclusion, Indigeneity and accessibility (EDIIA). With this in mind, our goal is a workforce that reflects the communities we serve. We welcome all qualified applicants to apply including individuals with disabilities, those who identify as Black, Indigenous or persons of colour, members of the LGBTQ2S+ community and others. Who We Are

AbleLiving is a not for profit organization operated by Thrive Group. Thrive Group has been created to bring like-minded organizations together to develop a broad range and continuum of services that enable clients to live fulfilling lives as independently as possible.

Who We Hire

At Thrive Group organizations, workplace culture matters. We believe that providing our employees with a supportive, healthy work environment means better quality service for our clients. Many Thrive Group employees describe what they do as “more than just a job”. Teams work collaboratively to enhance the lives of the people we serve and that creates a strong sense of satisfaction and pride.
For the past two years, Thrive Group has been certified as a GREAT WORKPLACE after a thorough and independent analysis conducted by Great Place to Work® Canada. This certification is based on direct feedback from employees, provided as part of an extensive and anonymous survey about the workplace experience.

If this sounds like the kind of organization you would like to be a part of, check out our job listings below. You can view postings for all Thrive Group organizations based on your skills and areas of interest.

About Brampton Library

As a progressive organization serving a diverse population of approximately 700,000, we enrich the lives of our residents by promoting literacy and providing access to recreational materials and information (books, magazines, large print materials, audiobooks, DVDs, and more) in our branches and online. The library also offers more than 110 computer workstations with free internet access and wireless connectivity for members' own laptops. Explore our website to learn more.

Brampton Library accepts resumes for employment on an ongoing basis. Resumes will be kept on file for six (6) months and are reviewed continuously as suitable vacancies come open to external applicants.

Please note that all job offers will be conditional upon providing, at your expense, a current Criminal Record Search acceptable for employment at the Library.

Brampton Library is a unionized environment, CUPE Local 1776.

Come Join Bruce Grey Child and Family Services!

BGCFS is committed to staffing a workforce representative of the diverse populations we serve. We encourage applicants from Indigenous, African Canadian, people with disabilities, 2SLGBTQ+ communities to apply and self identify in their cover letter. We are committed to creating an accessible environment for all. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

BGCFS has its main office in Owen Sound and an additional office in Walkerton. Bruce and Grey counties offer many kilometers of waterfront along Lake Huron and Georgian Bay. These two bodies of water are central attractions for the year round and seasonal residents. We are also a close drive to the Blue Mountains area.

BGCFS is committed to partnering with the communities to provide professional child welfare services, focused on promoting the best interests, protection, and well-being of children and youth. We work with parents, family members, and community organizations to promote and support families.
We are committed to equity, diversity, and inclusion in our workplace and our community. Click here to learn more.

Connect2Work for Newcomers
Program Description

At YMCA BC, we believe every newcomer youth deserves a strong start to their career journey in Canada. Connect2Work program is dedicated to bridging the gap between settlement and employment, offering the essential skills, coaching and connections you need to confidently build your future in the local job market.

Drop-in
Free
Group of 15-20
Y Membership Required: No
Connect2Work for Newcomers
Program overview

Connect2Work is a free, pre-employment program that supports newcomer youth (ages 15 to 30) and adults (30+) in developing essential life and job readiness skills. Participants take part in workshops, receive one-on-one employment coaching, build communication and English language skills, digital skills and gain access to networking opportunities.

This program is available at the Robert Lee YCMA centre in downtown Vancouver.
Program benefits
Weekly skill-building workshops led by experienced facilitators
Individual coaching to navigate resumes, interviews and Canadian workplace culture
ESL support to strengthen communication and workplace readiness
Networking opportunities with peers and local professionals
Supportive environment that encourages settlement confidence and self-advocacy
Program eligibility
Ages 15+
Must be a newcomer

Thanks for your interest in employment on the Springs Team!

Springs operates differently than many churches in that we distinguish very little difference between paid staff positions and volunteer positions. In fact, many of our volunteers are ministry leaders, but with unpaid positions. When we determine that a ministry role can only be filled by a paid staff person, we look to those people already plugged in and working on the Springs Team. This gives us a track record of the person already passionately labouring alongside us.

Please note: Springs keeps resumes on file for 3 months and while we appreciate your application, only those applicants that are being considered will be contacted.

To express an interest in working at Springs, please submit your resume along with a cover letter that clearly indicates your area of employment interest and specifically where you are currently volunteering at Springs to:

Join the Trans Canada Trail team!
 
Join us in continually improving the longest network of multi-use recreational trails in the world.
Who we are

The Trans Canada Trail (the Trail) is the longest inter-connected network of multi-use recreational trails in the world, spanning over 29,000 kilometres on land and water. Linking three oceans – the Atlantic, Pacific and Arctic – the Trail connects 900 municipalities across every province and territory. It is a ribbon that connects Canada’s diverse landscapes, seasons, people and experiences, and fosters unity, collaboration and connectedness.

Trans Canada Trail (the organization) is a registered charity that stewards this national trail in collaboration with community-based partners. With funding from the Government of Canada through Parks Canada, and investments from all levels of government and generous donors, Trans Canada Trail is the largest investor in trail infrastructure projects in Canada, supporting improvements, growth and enhancements for the benefit of generations to come.

Bridges of Canada has provided multi-faith chaplaincy services to Correctional Service Canada (CSC) since 2016, supporting the spiritual care and rehabilitation of individuals in federal institutions across Canada.

​

Through partnerships with faith communities nationwide, Bridges supports a broad range of spiritual traditions including Protestant, Roman Catholic, Indigenous spiritual practices, Muslim, Sikh, Jewish, Buddhist, Hindu, Wiccan, Rastafarian, and other faith groups. These services contribute to offender rehabilitation, institutional well-being, and successful reintegration into the community.

​

Bridges of Canada has also partnered with CSC on Faith Community Reintegration Projects (FCRP), helping support individuals transitioning from incarceration back into the community through faith-based mentorship, programming, and community support networks.

​

In Alberta, Bridges additionally provides provincial chaplaincy coordination services across correctional and remand facilities, helping ensure consistent access to spiritual and religious care for incarcerated individuals of all faith backgrounds.

Why Lead?

“Lead helps children and their families show the world what they can do.”

Our dynamic teams provide opportunities for children and families to learn through specialized programs in their home and community.  We support children and their families believing children learn best through play and within naturally occurring day-to-day activities.  Using an “I do, we do, you do” model for learning, we work through the stages of skill development together with children, families, and community partners.  Together, we develop strong, flexible programs.

We take pride in our positive workplace culture that is preserved through an environment of respect, collaboration, creativity and wellness.  This forms the foundation of the excellent work we do with families, children, community partners and each other.  We value what you will bring to our team and we want to be part of your professional journey.

Lead Foundation is committed to maximizing our staff’s personal and professional growth through:

A supportive and positive team of professionals and para-professionals
Flexible work hours and work environment
A competitive salary
An amazing benefits package that prioritizes staff wellness
Mileage and expenses reimbursement
Continuous professional development opportunities
A chance to have fun while making a difference in the lives of children and their families

CAREER OPPORTUNITIES

For 56 years, Aventa Addiction Treatment for Women has been offering alcohol, drug, gambling and nicotine addiction treatment for women.

Aventa offers a trauma-informed curriculum specifically designed for women with addictions, the treatment program has been researched and written by Dr. Stephanie Covington, a nationally recognized clinician, author and lecturer. Our treatment programs place an emphasis on providing an environment that is safe, supportive, and establishes a clear recovery process for women.

One of Aventa’s priorities is working with pregnant women; pregnant women are admitted without delay which helps decrease the risks associated with Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder (FASD).

Aventa is Accredited with Exemplary Standing from Accreditation Canada.

Aventa values diversity and is committed to employment equity in recruiting Aventa Board Members, Staff, and Volunteers.

We are hiring for the following positions:

Megan Acres - Rural Healthcare: The Next Generation

 

Investing in the Next Generation of Rural Healthcare

Over the past year, Community Foundation Grey Bruce Executive Director Stuart Reid has had the privilege of mentoring University of Western Ontario master's candidate Megan Acres as she completed her capstone project, Rural Healthcare: The Next Generation.

A lifelong resident of Meaford and graduate of Georgian Bay Community School, Megan has brought both academic expertise and personal insight to a research project focused on an important local challenge: how to encourage more Grey-Bruce students to pursue careers in healthcare. Using an equity lens, Megan's research examines the barriers and protective factors that influence whether students from Grey-Bruce pursue post-secondary education in healthcare fields. Her findings highlight several key factors that shape student decisions, including hands-on learning opportunities, financial considerations, transportation challenges, self-selection, and access to professional networks and mentorship (see graphic below).

The Comox Valley Community Foundation has served the Comox Valley since 1996. We work to bring together the generosity of donors with the ideas and energy of charities and communities across the Comox Valley. Join us and start making a difference in our community.

Why the Comox Valley?

The Comox Valley is considered one of the most desirable places to live in Canada due to its incredible natural setting on the Salish Sea, mild climate and limitless year-round recreational pursuits. An outdoor adventure and culinary destination, you can ski with an ocean view, swim in crystal clear waters, mountain bike or hike along one of the many river systems, paddle the many kilometres of stunning shoreline, or tee off at one of many scenic golf courses. The Comox Valley is also renowned for its many cultural, art, music, and food festivals.

Why the Comox Valley Community Foundation?

CVCF is an equal opportunity organization and encourages interest from equity deserving individuals, women, youth, individuals with disabilities, and all other qualified applicants. We welcome you to inform us, in confidence, if you require any special accommodations to participate in our recruitment process.

MUST LOVE CATS

We are seeking passionate individuals who share MEOW Foundation’s goals, mission and vision.

Our Vision
All communities caring for every cat with respect and compassion.

Our Mission
Champion the well-being of cats through humane programs, advocacy and education.

Our Goals

Work with the public to care for and provide shelter to stray, abandoned and feral cats in Calgary;
Provide the highest level of care to every cat who enters our programs and immediate veterinary treatment to sick or injured cats;
Match adoptable cats with loving families who will provide them with care and attention for the rest of their natural lives;
Promote responsible pet ownership, including the spaying/neutering of all cats and kittens; provision of nutritious food, water, shelter, love, regular health care and permanent identification such as a microchip or tattoo; licensing; and prevention from becoming a nuisance;
Reduce feline overpopulation and suffering;
Promote humane treatment, compassion and respect for all animals.
WHY WORK AT MEOW FOUNDATION?
We offer many benefits and perks including a worthwhile opportunity to work in the non-profit sector and feel a sense of personal fulfillment in the area of animal welfare
Work with a caring and knowledgeable team of staff and volunteers

A home is not made with bricks and mortar or wood and nails; a home is made with love, kindness, and respect. We could live in any building, anywhere in the world but if love is absent, happiness will evade us. NohKohWaa believes a home is where every child is valued and knows their worth.

Join the team at Smitty’s™ where hospitality isn’t just a job, it’s our passion. With over 80 locations across Canada, we invite those with a natural gift for creating exceptional guest experiences to embark on their next career journey with us.

You'll be part of a cherished Canadian dining tradition. Starting from a single location in Calgary, AB, Smitty’s™ has grown into a thriving franchise with over 80 locations nationwide. About Us

Sport for Life is a Victoria-based non-profit that aims to mobilize quality sport and physical literacy development to the world. It is recognized as the global experts on the Canadian Sport for Life Movement, Long-Term Development in Sport and Physical Activity, and physical literacy development. By bridging gaps between sectors, creating new collaborations with Canadian institutions, and mobilizing our knowledge to communities across the country, we aim to create a future in which absolutely everyone has access to quality sport and physical literacy experiences.

Sport for Life strives to be a catalyst for change and aspires to create equitable, diverse, inclusive, and accessible opportunities for everyone not only in sport and physical activity, but also in the workplace. Our talented and diverse team, which is spread out across Canada, is dedicated to a common vision and a set of shared core values. Our workspace is inclusive, friendly and team-based, and we walk our talk by enabling our employees to live the most active lives possible.

To read more about who we are and what we do, visit our About Us page.

Friendliness – We value friendliness and positivity in all of our daily interactions among employees and, of course, our customers.
Teamwork – We strive to create a positive work environment where each member feels valued and supported. A strong and cohesive team is the backbone of a growing and successful business.
Creativity/Creative Thinking – We value creativity in many forms. From creating unique, delicious flavors and beautifully decorated products to thinking outside the box when it comes to taking on tasks, solving problems, and meeting challenges.

Our culture

We are proud to foster a workplace that values compassion, inclusion and diversity. We welcome and encourage all applicants who want to help shape the future of children’s health — with No Bounds.

We are a husband and wife team baking our dreams.  Our names are Isaac and Myroslava. We romanticized about opening up our own bakery and café when we first met in 2010. At that time, Isaac was working as a ceramicist and Myro was baking bread at a bakery in the downtown area. We loved our simple life but we wanted to do more. We wanted to find a way to channel both of our passions into meaningful work that was rooted in quality, passion and most importantly community. The overriding goal of our human resources policy is to bring together a professional team, capable of implementing large-scale projects around the world.

The candidate selection process consists of several stages. For the first stage, the applicant is required to submit a CV detailing previous work experience, key projects implemented, education to date, and level of foreign language proficiency. For the second stage, candidates may be called on to demonstrate their competence by undergoing a test. This will also provide potential employees with some insight into their professional competence. During the final stage of the interview process, candidates attend one or more meetings which are aimed at identifying the candidate’s communicative and technical skills, as well as their level of proficiency in foreign languages. To apply for a job in one of the Uranium One companies, please submit your CV to hr@u1holding.com.
